question_title: How many commercial aircraft deliveries will Airbus report for April 2026 ?
summary: Airbus April 2026 delivery forecast based on historical April deliveries (54 in 2023, 61 in 2024, 56 in 2025), seasonal fraction analysis (April averages ~7.5% of annual total), and the 2026 outlook (~900 deliveries expected by analysts, representing ~13.5% YoY growth from 793 in 2025). The slow January 2026 start (only 19 deliveries, slowest this decade) introduces downside risk from persistent supply chain issues, but January is always the weakest month and historically is not strongly predictive of April. Monte Carlo simulation using three weighted scenarios: successful ramp-up (40%), 2025-like pace (40%), and supply chain drag (20%). Median estimate ~61, consistent with scaled historical April fraction applied to a moderately achievable annual target.

factors.description: Historical April deliveries have been remarkably stable: 54 (2023), 61 (2024), 56 (2025), averaging 57 with low variance
Analysts project ~900 Airbus deliveries in 2026 (+13.5% YoY), which would scale April to ~63.5 using average seasonal fraction of 7.5%
January 2026 was only 19 deliveries (slowest start this decade, -24% vs Jan 2025), potentially signaling persistent supply chain issues
Airbus has not yet formally announced a 2026 delivery target, adding uncertainty to the ramp-up trajectory
Massive backlog of 8,777 aircraft provides strong demand support - delivery bottleneck is supply-side, not demand
Fuselage panel quality issue from late 2025 required delivery target cut; lingering effects could persist into early-mid 2026

reasoning:
**Airbus April 2026 Delivery Forecast Summary:**

| Percentile | Deliveries |
|-----------|-----------|
| P10 | 47 |
| P20 | 52 |
| P40 | 58 |
| P60 | 64 |
| P80 | 69 |
| P90 | 73 |

**Key reasoning:**
- **Historical Aprils**: 54 (2023), 61 (2024), 56 (2025) — stable mid-50s to low-60s range
- **2026 ramp-up**: Analysts expect ~900 annual deliveries (+13.5% YoY), which would scale April to ~63-64 if the seasonal pattern holds
- **Downside risk**: January 2026's 19 deliveries was the slowest start this decade, reflecting lingering supply chain issues from the late-2025 fuselage panel quality problem
- **Upside case**: Massive backlog (8,777 aircraft) means demand isn't the constraint — if supply chains normalize, deliveries could push into the upper 60s-70s
- **Median estimate**: ~61 aircraft, reflecting a blend of historical pattern and moderate growth expectations tempered by the weak start to 2026
